satire_attempt: Today on Things I Tell Junior Engineers: every component is born, every component dies, every component is reborn as a slightly different YAML config. Buy my course on Stoic Software Architecture, where I will teach you that legacy code is just code that has lived enough to know its own mortality.
verdict_reason: The satire requires the verse to be sentimental about the cycle, but the verse explicitly is not — it treats the cycle as structural and operationalizes it as a lineage check. The five-generation cache example resists the satirical reading because it is too specific to be platitudinous.
